prompt:
Our home-game league runs an online series and I'm sick of arguing about who won a pot after the fact — I want a hand evaluator we can trust as the referee. Go module go-pokereval, package pokereval at the root, stdlib only.

Cards: Card{Rank int, Suit byte}, rank 2..14 with 11/12/13/14 = J/Q/K/A, suit one of 'c' 'd' 'h' 's'. ParseCard reads the usual two-char notation — rank char from 23456789TJQKA, then the suit letter, so "As", "Td", "2c" — and rejects anything else (no "10c", no lowercase ranks). ParseCards splits on whitespace. Card.String() gives the canonical form back.

Evaluation: Eval7(cards []Card) (Hand, error) takes EXACTLY seven distinct cards (community + hole, error otherwise) and returns the best five-card hand. Hand is {Category Category, Ranks []int}. Category is an int type ordered weakest to strongest — HighCard, OnePair, TwoPair, ThreeOfAKind, Straight, Flush, FullHouse, FourOfAKind, StraightFlush — whose String() forms are "high-card", "one-pair", "two-pair", "three-of-a-kind", "straight", "flush", "full-house", "four-of-a-kind", "straight-flush". Ranks is the tiebreak vector, most significant first: pairs/trips/quads list the group rank(s) then kickers descending, two-pair is high pair / low pair / kicker, full house is trips then pair, straights are just the high card (the wheel A-2-3-4-5 counts, high card 5), flush and high-card are the five ranks descending. Compare(a, b Hand) returns -1/0/+1 and suits must never break a tie.

Showdown(community []Card, holes [][]Card) (winners []int, hands []Hand, err error): five community cards, two hole cards per player, every card distinct across the whole deal or it's an error; returns each player's evaluated hand plus the ascending indices of everyone entitled to a share of the pot — chopped pots are a fact of life and MUST come back as multiple winners.

testdata/deals.txt holds real disputes from our league nights (format: community | holes separated by ';'). pokereval_test.go is the acceptance contract — it pins those showdowns, a pile of exact category+kicker evaluations, and all the classic traps (three pairs on seven cards, six-card flushes, the board playing, quads out of a paired board). go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass without touching the test file or testdata/.
